LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHININ TANA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AND DENIED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUBLICATION OF INJURIOUS FALSEHOOD, FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SLANDER OF TITLE, THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT. A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE REQUIRES PROOF THAT THE ALLEGED ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP WAS DISRUPTED. THERE IS NO SUCH EVIDENCE HERE. AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN AND/OR CONSPIRED WITH OTHERS TO ENGAGE IN THE WRONGDOING ALLEGED, WHETHER THE COMMUNICATIONS MADE BY DEFENDANTS WERE MADE IN GOOD FAITH AND WITHOUT MALICE, WHETHER THOSE COMMUNICATIONS MADE THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS ALLEGED IN THE SIXTH CLAIM MORE EXPENSIVE OR DIFFICULT, AND WHETHER THOSE COMMUNICATIONS CAUSED OTHER DAMAGES TO THE PLAINTIFFS. MOREOVER, AS TO THE CLAIM OF SLANDER OF TITLE, WHILE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE, THAT DEFENDANT, DIRECTLY OR BY CONSPIRING WITH OTHERS, SLANDERED THE TITLE TO THE PLAINTIFFS' ENTITIES, THE FOURTH CLAIM ALLEGES DISPARAGEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' TITLE TO PROPERTY OWNED BY THEM, AND THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THAT POINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAFAEL PACQUING'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AND DENIED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUBLICATION OF INJURIOUS FALSEHOOD, FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SLANDER OF TITLE, THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT. A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE REQUIRES PROOF THAT THE ALLEGED ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP WAS DISRUPTED. THERE IS NO SUCH EVIDENCE HERE. AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN AND/OR CONSPIRED WITH OTHERS TO ENGAGE IN THE WRONGDOING ALLEGED, WHETHER THE COMMUNICATIONS MADE BY DEFENDANTS WERE MADE IN GOOD FAITH AND WITHOUT MALICE, WHETHER THOSE COMMUNICATIONS MADE THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS ALLEGED IN THE SIXTH CLAIM MORE EXPENSIVE OR DIFFICULT, AND WHETHER THOSE COMMUNICATIONS CAUSED OTHER DAMAGES TO THE PLAINTIFFS. MOREOVER, AS TO THE CLAIM OF SLANDER OF TITLE, WHILE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE, THAT DEFENDANT, DIRECTLY OR BY CONSPIRING WITH OTHERS, SLANDERED THE TITLE TO THE PLAINTIFFS' ENTITIES, THE FOURTH CLAIM ALLEGES DISPARAGEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' TITLE TO PROPERTY OWNED BY THEM, AND THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THAT POINT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEFFREY CHANG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AND DENIED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUBLICATION OF INJURIOUS FALSEHOOD, FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SLANDER OF TITLE, THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT. A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE REQUIRES PROOF THAT THE ALLEGED ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP WAS DISRUPTED. THERE IS NO SUCH EVIDENCE HERE. AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN AND/OR CONSPIRED WITH OTHERS TO ENGAGE IN THE WRONGDOING ALLEGED, WHETHER THE COMMUNICATIONS MADE BY DEFENDANTS WERE MADE IN GOOD FAITH AND WITHOUT MALICE, WHETHER THOSE COMMUNICATIONS MADE THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS ALLEGED IN THE SIXTH CLAIM MORE EXPENSIVE OR DIFFICULT, AND WHETHER THOSE COMMUNICATIONS CAUSED OTHER DAMAGES TO THE PLAINTIFFS. MOREOVER, AS TO THE CLAIM OF SLANDER OF TITLE, WHILE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE, THAT DEFENDANT, DIRECTLY OR BY CONSPIRING WITH OTHERS, SLANDERED THE TITLE TO THE PLAINTIFFS' ENTITIES, THE FOURTH CLAIM ALLEGES DISPARAGEMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' TITLE TO PROPERTY OWNED BY THEM, AND THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THAT POINT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HERMAN KWAI MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AND DENIED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PUBLICATION OF INJURIOUS FALSEHOOD, FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SLANDER OF TITLE, THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT. A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE REQUIRES PROOF THAT THE ALLEGED ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP WAS DISRUPTED. THERE IS NO SUCH EVIDENCE HERE. AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN AND/OR CONSPIRED WITH OTHERS TO ENGAGE IN THE WRONGDOING ALLEGED. MOREOVER, AS TO THE CLAIM OF SLANDER OF TITLE, WHILE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE, THAT DEFENDANT, DIRECTLY OR BY CONSPIRING WITH OTHERS, SLANDERED THE TITLE TO THE PLAINTIFFS' ENTITIES, THE FOURTH CLAIM ALLEGES SLANDER TO THE TITLE OF PROPERTY OWNED BY THOSE ENTITIES, AND THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THAT POINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HERMAN KWAI'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL IS DENIED. DEFENDANT NEVER HAD AN ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED RULE THAT THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE REMAINS WITH THE OWNER OF A CORPORATION IN BANKRUPTCY WHEN THE COURT PIERCES THAT CORPORATION'S VEIL WOULD FRUSTRATE BANKRUPTCY LAW AND PREVENT THE TRUSTEE FROM MAXIMIZING THE VALUE OF THE ESTATE. (COMMODITIES FUTURES TRADING COMM. V. WEINTRAUB, 471 U.S. 343, 353). MOREOVER, THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY AND IT APPEARS THT ANY LEGITIMATE CONCERN THAT DEFENDANT MIGHT HAVE SHOULD HAVE BEEN RAISED WITH THE BANKRUPTCY COURT IN THE CONTEXT OF OBJECTING TO THE TRUSTEE'S SELECTION OF COUNSEL, AND NOT THIS COURT. Motion To Disqualify Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOY HUANG, ESQ. AND FREDERICK GEONETTA, ESQ., GEONETTA & FRUCHT, 415.433.4589 AND 415.421.4770, FOR PLAINTIFFS BURLINGAME INVESTMENT CORPORATION ET AL.
JOHN CAREY, ESQ., WILD CAREY & FIFE, 415.837.3101, FOR DEFENDANT KWAI.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT HERMAN KWAI'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel is denied. Defendant never had an attorney-client relationship with plaintiff's counsel. Additionally, defendant's proposed rule that the attorney-client privilege remains with the owner of a corporation in bankruptcy when the court pierces that corporation's veil would frustrate bankruptcy law and prevent the trustee from maximizing the value of the estate. (Commodities Futures Trading Comm. v. Weintraub, 471 U.S. 343, 353). Moreover, the motion is untimely and it appears tht any legitimate concern that defendant might have should have been raised with the bankruptcy court in the context of objecting to the trustee's selection of counsel, and not this court. (counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chinin Tana, Esq., of Law Office of Chinin Tana, Tel. 415.788.8811, Defendant and moving party appearing on his own behalf.
John E. Carey, Esq., of Wild, Carey & Fife, Tel. 415.837.3101, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Fred Geonetta, Esq., Tel. 415.421.4770, and Joy Huang, Esq., Tel. 415.433.4589, of Geonetta & Frucht LLP, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHININ TANA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted as to the seventh cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage and denied as to the third cause of action for publication of injurious falsehood, fourth cause of action for slander of title, the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and the sixth cause of action for intentional interference with contract. A claim for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage requires proof that the alleged economic relationship was disrupted. There is no such evidence here. As to all other claims, there are triable issues whether defendant engaged in and/or conspired with others to engage in the wrongdoing alleged, whether the communications made by defendants were made in good faith and without malice, whether those communications made the contractual relations alleged in the sixth claim more expensive or difficult, and whether those communications caused other damages to the plaintiffs. Moreover, as to the claim of slander of title, while there is no evidence, that defendant, directly or by conspiring with others, slandered the title to the plaintiffs' entities, the fourth claim alleges disparagement of plaintiffs' title to property owned by them, and there is a triable issue on that point. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
